summary: Small TY subsystem matching-improvement slice split from PR #2617. The loaded diff changes `src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c` and `src/melee/ty/tylist.c`, mostly by adjusting local lifetime/declaration order and reverting some semantic-looking struct-field accesses to offset-style fields. The decomp-dev report records two unmatched-item improvements: `fn_80313BD8` from 97.03% to 98.40% and `un_8031BC54` from 99.51% to 99.73%. No human review comments were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Adjusted `un_8031BC54` by introducing local `id = arg0`, using `id` for later calls, and moving `TyDspArchNames` declarations after other locals.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report item `main/melee/ty/tydisplay` `un_8031BC54` +2 bytes, 99.51% to 99.73%

key_files.role: Adjusted `fn_80313BD8` field accesses and locals: `state->jobj` to `state->x288`, `entries[0]`-based accesses to `x274`/`x270`, removed temporary `idx`, and `entries[10].links[0]` to `x278`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report item `main/melee/ty/tylist` `fn_80313BD8` +27 bytes, 97.03% to 98.40%

what_changed.text: `un_8031BC54` now preserves `arg0` in a local `s32 id` and uses that local for `un_8031B9DC` and `un_8031BF34`.
`un_8031BC54` local declaration order changed by moving `TyDspArchNames jobj_names` and `matanim_names` below `TyDspBgData* data`.
`fn_80313BD8` now uses offset-style state fields (`x288`, `x274`, `x270`, `x278`) in places that previously used semantic-looking fields (`jobj`, `entries[0]`, `entries[10].links[0]`).
`fn_80313BD8` removes a short-lived `s16 idx` local and directly reads `state->x270->idx` in the comparison and casted call argument.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed `s16 idx`; changed to `(s8) state->x270->idx`

smart_moves.text: The PR was scoped as an isolated directory/subsystem slice under `src/melee/ty`, making a small matching-improvement diff easier to review than the larger source branch.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review.`

smart_moves.text: The PR body distinguishes full unsplit-branch scores from the authoritative per-PR CI/decomp-dev slice report, reducing overclaiming from source-branch numbers.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes.`
text_corpus: decomp-dev report with 2 unmatched-item improvements

smart_moves.text: The changes are narrow and tied to two reported functions, avoiding broad TY subsystem churn in the loaded diff.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists only `fn_80313BD8` and `un_8031BC54`

decomp_lessons.text: For near-matching functions, preserving an argument in a local alias can be a useful codegen tactic when later calls need the same value, but it should remain narrowly justified by objdiff/report evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `s32 id = arg0`; later calls use `id`

decomp_lessons.text: Semantic-looking struct field names can be premature in unmatched code. In this slice, reverting `jobj`/`entries[...]` accesses to offset-style fields corresponded with an improved `fn_80313BD8` score, so curators should treat this as an example of conservative naming rather than proof of field semantics.

naming_conventions.text: When Ty list state field meaning is not proven, offset-style names such as `x270`, `x274`, `x278`, and `x288` may be preferable to semantic names like `jobj` or `entries`.
The local name `id` was introduced for a copied `s32 arg0` value in `un_8031BC54`; evidence supports only this local tactic, not a broader naming rule for the subsystem.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Local declaration ordering changed in `un_8031BC54`, with `TyDspArchNames` declarations moved below pointer/data locals.
Argument copy tactic: `arg0` is copied into `id`, and later function calls use the copy.
Temporary-local removal tactic: `s16 idx` was removed from `fn_80313BD8`, with direct field reads used instead.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed `s16 idx`; direct use of `state->x270->idx`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Field-access reshaping tactic: semantic-looking nested accesses were replaced with direct offset-style state fields in `fn_80313BD8`.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported 2 improvements in unmatched items: `main/melee/ty/tylist` `fn_80313BD8` +27 bytes, 97.03% to 98.40%; `main/melee/ty/tydisplay` `un_8031BC54` +2 bytes, 99.51% to 99.73%.
No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: The loaded changed-file metadata lists only `src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c` and `src/melee/ty/tylist.c`; the PR body also lists `src/melee/ty/toy.c`, but no loaded changed-file entry or diff hunk for `toy.c` is present.
The PR body's broad preliminary score report is explicitly for the full unsplit source branch, not necessarily this exact PR slice; the decomp-dev bot report is stronger evidence for this slice.
No build log, objdiff details, or human review reasoning are included beyond the automated decomp-dev summary.
The diff supports codegen/matching tactics and field-name changes, but it does not prove final struct semantics for the Ty list state fields.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Conservative field naming in TY list matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2629 is a concrete example where reverting semantic-looking `TyList` state accesses (`jobj`, `entries[...]`) to offset-style fields (`x288`, `x274`, `x270`, `x278`) was part of a reported improvement to `fn_80313BD8`. Use as supporting evidence for conservative naming when field semantics are unproven, not as proof of the offset fields' meanings.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow local lifetime and declaration-order adjustments can improve near matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2629 shows small local-shape changes in `un_8031BC54`—copying `arg0` to `id` and changing declaration order—associated with a reported +2 byte unmatched-item improvement. Keep this as a narrow tactic example requiring verifier evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2629 as an example under conservative naming or matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2629 as an example that semantic-looking recovered struct fields should not outrank matching evidence when field meaning is not proven. The diff changed `state->jobj`/`state->entries[...]` back to offset-style fields in `fn_80313BD8`, and the decomp-dev report showed an improvement for that function.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a candidate standards example, not a new accepted rule; existing standards already cover conservative naming and evidence-backed tactics.

source_update_candidates.title: TY slice PR #2629 touched tydisplay and tylist
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2629's loaded changed-file evidence covers `src/melee/ty/tydisplay.c` and `src/melee/ty/tylist.c`; the PR body also mentions `toy.c`, but the loaded diff/changed-file metadata does not include a `toy.c` change.
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may want to record the discrepancy between PR body file list and loaded changed-file evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the full preliminary branch report numbers as verified slice outcomes; the PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative for the slice.
Do not infer final meanings for `x270`, `x274`, `x278`, or `x288`; the diff only shows that these offset-style fields were used in the improved source.
Do not record human reviewer intent; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not claim `toy.c` changed in this slice from loaded diff evidence; it appears only in the PR body file list.
